task_completion.description: Create a clinical evaluation for patient John Zenon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a GNU Health 'Patient Evaluation' or 'Clinical Evaluation' window with the patient name 'John Zenon' selected.

task_completion.description: Record the hypothermic core body temperature.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the temperature field within the clinical evaluation showing a value of 35.5 or lower (e.g., 35.2).
Look for a diagnosis entry in the evaluation or patient record showing the ICD-10 code 'T68' (Hypothermia) or 'T33' (Superficial frostbite).

task_completion.description: Order at least 2 baseline laboratory tests for tissue damage.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Lab Test' or 'Laboratory Order' screen showing at least two distinct tests (e.g., CPK, CMP, CBC) requested for John Zenon.

task_completion.description: Prescribe an analgesic for rewarming pain.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Prescription' or 'Medication' record showing an analgesic (e.g., Ibuprofen, Ketorolac, Acetaminophen, or Aspirin) prescribed to John Zenon.

task_completion.description: Schedule a tissue viability follow-up appointment for 2 to 7 days from today.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an 'Appointment' record for John Zenon with a scheduled date that is 2 to 7 days after the system's current date.

integrity.description: The agent used the GNU Health graphical user interface to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the GNU Health client interface (menus, tabs, forms) being actively used, rather than terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type the required information into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Information must be entered into the appropriate GNU Health form fields, not a generic text editor (like gedit) or note-taking app.

integrity.description: The agent selected diagnoses, lab tests, and medications from the system's database.
integrity.visual_evidence: Diagnoses, labs, and medications should appear as selected records from the system (often indicated by a magnifying glass, folder, or link icon next to the field in GNU Health), rather than just free-text typed into a generic notes field.
